mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS holds above 5.0% for three consecutive months without further hikes, markets price in an end to the tightening cycle, compressing the equity risk premium and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as investors anticipate a future pivot.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and unchanged for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 5.08% in June 2023 vintage
regime_hint: monetary tightening plateau near terminal rate
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during plateau periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional 60-day return distribution (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects expanding aggregate income and consumption capacity, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth months show forward 20-day SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from negative growth months (difference-in-means p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) signals a broad-based consumer and investment demand expansion that neither series alone captures; this combined demand impulse drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month because it indicates durable-goods and services spending momentum feeding into corporate revenue.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised value 673902 vs vintage 686573 in 2023-05-01
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST are jointly accelerating, SPX forward 20-day returns fail to exceed the unconditional positive-return base rate of 65.5% by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO exceeding 0.5% over two consecutive months signals contraction in industrial output and impending earnings weakness for cyclical and manufacturing sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as markets price in slower supply-side growth.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised down from 103.0107 to 100.9286 for 2023-05-01
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following triggered INDPRO declines are positive in more than 55% of instances, or rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary regime where CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases fall below 0.3% while FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 5%, markets interpret the combination as a soft-landing signal, expecting future rate cuts without a recession, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised value 304.014 vs vintage 303.841 for 2023-06-01
failure_condition: During periods meeting the joint CPI/FEDFUNDS condition, SPX 60-day forward returns underperform the unconditional mean, or the effect reverses sign in more than one of three 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points from its prior 12-month trough (a Sahm-rule-like threshold) signals the onset of labor market deterioration and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=3.6% in June 2023, near historic lows
regime_hint: labor market turning point / early recession signal
failure_condition: Following a triggered UNRATE rise, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, indicating the threshold does not reliably precede downturns.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces modest positive S&P 500 returns over the following week regardless of the broader macro regime.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive RSAFS growth months are not significantly different from returns following flat/negative RSAFS months (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both post positive month-over-month growth simultaneously, it signals synchronized expansion in production and employment that reduces near-term recession probability, causing a short-lived positive S&P 500 reaction over the next week as growth data beats cautious expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM > 0 AND PAYEMS MoM > 0 in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised down from 156204 to 155880 for 2023-06-01
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following joint INDPRO/PAYEMS positive growth are not significantly higher than the unconditional 5-day return mean (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.
